name: Frijoles Puercos, Michoacan
recipeIngredient: 2 oz Bacon, cut into small squares
4 Canned large chiles or 4 Canned jalapenos en escabeche to taste
1/4 lb Queso añejo, Chihuahua, Romano or Meunster cheese
2 oz Chicarron (fried crackling), broken into small pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Fry bacon in a small skillet over low heat until the fat renders out. Do not let the bacon brown too much. Remove the bacon pieces with a slotted spoon and set aside.
2. Add the crumbled chorizo to the bacon fat. Cook gently without browning for about 5 minutes. Remove the chorizo with a slotted spoon and set aside.
3. In the same fat, fry the tortilla squares until they are completely crisp. Drain on paper towels.
4. Put the Frijoles de Olla (beans) in a heavy, wide pan and add the whole chiles, bacon, and chorizo. Heat gently so the beans do not scorch at the bottom.
5. When the beans begin to bubble, add the cheese and chicarron. As the cheese begins to melt, sprinkle the top of the beans with the tortilla squares.
6. Serve immediately before the squares become soft.
